one_time_description: Flood Settlement - Federal ($65,127,373) and Provincial ($19,839,609) received, with $83,082,612 transferred to community trust, net $500,000 Drinking Water Settlement retained; ISC Loan repayment of $1,884,370 also recorded as other expense
economic_notes: First Nation receives 65% of revenue from ISC (up from 47% prior year). Investment in Niiwin Wendaanimok Construction Limited Partnership ($400,001) made during year. Gas station under construction on hold (carrying value $310,265). Flood and Drinking Water settlements received and transferred to community trust. US tariff uncertainty noted as subsequent event. Legal claim filed over Anicinabe Park ownership.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to determine Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) liabilities under PS 3280. Large flood settlements ($65.1M federal + $19.8M provincial) received and transferred to community trust ($83.1M), with net $500K Drinking Water Settlement retained - these are significant one-time items. Accounts payable includes $5,243,414 flood settlement due to members. Statement dated April 14, 2025 for a March 31, 2023 year-end, indicating very late filing. Capital segment expenses of $727,821 and Economic Development of $500,439 also noted. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text. Province is Ontario. Line of credit of $1,858,533 for Community Centre construction included in current liabilities.
